domain: an independent IT and security consultant's business and personal life

turns.text: Signed my first big retainer of the year. A regional bank called Halverson Group brought me on to run their security program.
Congrats on landing Halverson Group. Running a whole security program is a meaty engagement.
Yeah, it's a twelve-month retainer. They're my anchor client for the year.
I'm not solo anymore. My business partner Marisol came on board to handle the compliance and audit side of the practice.
Having Marisol cover compliance and audit frees you up. How do you split the work?
I take the technical engagements, she takes the paperwork-heavy ones. We co-own the firm fifty-fifty.
Had coffee with my old mentor Devraj. He's the one who convinced me to go independent five years ago.
Mentors like Devraj are gold. Is he still consulting himself?
He's winding his own practice down, mostly giving advice now. Always picks up when I call.
Started building a little side product on weekends. It's a credential-rotation tool I'm calling GateKeep.
GateKeep sounds useful for exactly the clients you serve. Is it just for you or a real product?
Half scratch-my-own-itch, half maybe-a-business. We'll see if GateKeep goes anywhere.
New logo signed: a fintech startup called Nordic Vault wants a readiness assessment before their Series B.
Nordic Vault doing diligence before a raise is smart. What's the scope of the assessment?
Two weeks, mostly architecture review. Good short engagement to balance the long Halverson one.
Adopted a scruffy terrier mix from the rescue this weekend. Named him Biscuit.
Welcome home, Biscuit! A terrier mix will keep you on your toes.
He's already claimed the chair in my home office. Total menace, total love.
Finally kicking off the kitchen renovation I've put off for two years. Hired a contractor named Otto to run it.
A kitchen reno is a big project. Is Otto handling the whole thing end to end?
Design through demo. Otto says six weeks if the cabinets show up on time.
Rough week. Halverson Group got hit by a ransomware attack on Tuesday night.
That's serious. How bad was the ransomware hit at Halverson Group, and is the response under control?
Contained by Thursday thanks to the backups we'd set up. Brutal but it validated the program.
Nordic Vault passed with flying colors. They specifically needed to satisfy SOC 2 Type II for their investors.
Achieving SOC 2 Type II readiness ahead of a raise is a strong signal to Nordic Vault's investors.
Exactly. The report goes straight into their data room. Clean engagement.
Marisol finished a credential I didn't know she was working toward. She passed the CISA exam this month.
Passing the CISA is no small feat. That deepens the audit side of your firm a lot.
It does. Now Marisol can sign off on certain audits herself instead of subcontracting them.
Flew out to Reykjavik this week to do the on-site portion for Nordic Vault.
Reykjavik for an on-site with Nordic Vault! How was it being there in person?
Cold and gorgeous. Their whole engineering team is in Iceland, so it had to be in person.
Big personnel change at Halverson Group. They poached my main day-to-day contact, so my new point of contact there is a woman named Freya.
A new point of contact can reset a relationship. What's Freya like to work with?
Sharp, decisive. Freya runs their whole IT org, so decisions move faster now.
My mentor took a new gig. After years of advising me, Devraj joined a venture firm as an operating partner.
Devraj going in-house at a venture firm is a big shift. Which firm did he land at?
A shop called Hearthstone Ventures. He'll be vetting their security investments now.
The weekend project got real. A larger security vendor acquired GateKeep for an undisclosed sum.
An acquisition of GateKeep — congratulations! Who picked it up?
A company called Sentinel Labs. They want it as a feature in their platform. Surreal week.
The renovation hit a wall — my contractor bailed mid-project. I brought in a new contractor named Greta to finish the kitchen.
Losing a contractor mid-job is painful. Is Greta able to pick up where things were left?
She's untangling the mess now. Greta thinks two more weeks and the kitchen is finally done.
Got the green light to overhaul the access model. Freya picked the new direction: a zero-trust architecture across the whole org.
Moving to zero-trust is a major modernization. Good that Freya backed it decisively.
She pushed it through in a single meeting. Rollout starts next quarter.
My partner is taking the lead on a brand-new service line for the firm: a virtual CISO offering for small banks.
A virtual CISO line for small banks fits your niche perfectly. Is that Marisol's initiative?
All hers. She's productizing what we already do ad hoc. Could become our biggest revenue stream.
Took Biscuit to the lake for the first time today. He was terrified of the water for about ten minutes, then wouldn't get out.
Classic dog arc — terror to obsession in ten minutes. Sounds like a good day at the lake.
Slept the whole drive home. Best kind of tired.

queries.question: What type of attack hit my anchor client Halverson Group?
Which professional credential did my business partner Marisol pass?
Which venture firm did my mentor Devraj join?
Which company acquired my side product GateKeep?
Which compliance standard did the fintech client Nordic Vault need to satisfy?
In which city did I do the on-site work for the Nordic Vault engagement?
Which security architecture did Freya, my contact at the anchor client, choose for the bank?
Who is the contractor currently finishing my kitchen renovation?
Who is my current main point of contact at the anchor client Halverson Group?
What is my mentor Devraj's most recent job?
What do I know about my business partner Marisol?
What do I know about the Nordic Vault engagement?
What do I know about my side product GateKeep?
What did I name the dog I adopted from the rescue?
Which room of my house am I renovating?
How long is the retainer with my anchor client?
